brief_summary: Four-cap side-profile silhouette strip showing relative height and top-scoop shape. Cherry (~8mm, gentle scoop), OEM (~11mm, sculpted), SA (~16-18mm spherical dish), MT3 (~16mm deep cylindrical scoop). All on a shared baseline labelled 'PCB'. Amber accent traces the scoop curve of each cap. Caption: 'two axes do most of the work: sculpt and height'.
data_sources: keycap-profiles-compared.mdx § 'The two axes that actually matter' — 'A low-profile cap like Cherry sits roughly 8mm tall at the front edge. A tall profile like SA or MT3 sits closer to 16mm'
keycap-profiles-compared.mdx § 'Cherry' — 'It is low ... gently scooped top'
keycap-profiles-compared.mdx § 'OEM' — 'sculpted like Cherry, but a few millimeters taller and with slightly steeper side walls'
keycap-profiles-compared.mdx § 'SA' — 'tall, sculpted, and spherical — the top of each cap is a small dished sphere ... SA's row-one cap can sit close to 18mm tall'
keycap-profiles-compared.mdx § 'MT3' — 'sculpted like SA, but the scoop on top of each cap is a deep cylindrical depression'
warnings: OEM ~11mm is a representative midpoint, not an article-stated number — drawn between Cherry's 8mm and SA's 16mm to honor the article's 'a few millimeters taller than Cherry' framing
